subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: Β2.1.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the attributes of God
indicator_description: Mention the attributes of God that reveal His nature as Sustainer of life
examplars:
1. In groups, learners mention the attributes of God as sustainer of life: i. Giver of rain and sunshine, ii. The One who makes plants grow, iii. The Giver of air.
2. Learners mention these attributes of God in their local languages.
3. Learners talk about the attributes of God relevant to their daily lives, e.g. God gives life, rain and air.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.1.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of self as a unique individual
indicator_description: Identify things to do to develop a sense of self identity and self-worth
examplars:
1. Learners talk about themselves individually and appreciate the way God created them e.g. 'I am black and beautiful, I like the way God created me. I have great qualities'.
2. Learners role play scenarios and engage in activities that teach them the importance of developing a strong self-identity.
3. The need to feel special, more worthy and valued, more confident, more optimistic in order to do well in school and in life in general.
4. Learners compose poems/write essays about themselves and what they can do to feel more valued at home, school and the community.
5. Learners are assigned projects to dialogue with parents on how they can be supported and encouraged to build strong sense of identity e.g. encourage good behaviour and performance at home.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.1.3.1. Show appreciation of the roles of members of the family in their daily lives
indicator_description: Identify the role and responsibilities of the individual members of the family
examplars:
1. Learners, in groups, talk about their roles and roles of their parents in the family: Parents: Provision of shelter, food, security and education; Children: running errands, performing house chores, etc.
2. Learners role play the functions of the various members in the family, e.g. learners play the role of a father, mother, etc.
3. Teacher encourages learners to undertake their routine responsibilities without being commanded or prompted.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.1.4.1. Demonstrate understanding of the need to obey rules
indicator_description: Explain the need for obeying rules in the home and school
examplars:
1. Learners work in groups to talk about rules in the home, school and community.
2. Learners role play scenarios on obeying rules in the home, school and community.
3. Learners compose rhymes on obeying rules in the home and school.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: Β2.2.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of keeping the environment clean and safe
indicator_description: Describe ways of making the environment clean and safe
examplars:
1. Take a nature walk of the school environment and observe things in the environment-man made and natural: water, trees, stones, animals, buildings, cars, tables, chairs, etc.
2. Learners talk about things they can do to make the environment clean and safe e.g. planting trees, proper waste disposal, avoid bush burning, avoid defecating in water bodies, legal mining.
3. Learners identify ways of protecting land, e.g. do not throw polythene bags, water sachet, empty cans on the land.
4. Learners draw and colour a clean environment.
5. Learners draw people who keep our communities safe e.g. the police, fire service personnel.
6. Learners demonstrate safe practices in the community, e.g. sweeping school compound, picking litter.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.2.1.2. Demonstrate understanding of elements of the weather
indicator_description: Explain the elements of weather
examplars:
1. Learners explain the elements of weather, e.g. temperature, rainfall/precipitation, humidity, wind and cloud cover.
2. Learners draw weather chart using symbols.
3. Learners watch videos or pictures of different weather conditions.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.2.2.1. Recognise important plants and animals in the community
indicator_description: Explore the important plants in the community
examplars:
1. Learners identify and talk about crops like cocoa, yam, maize, shea butter, etc, and talk about why they are important to the community. e.g. they give us money and food.
2. Learners see the real crops, watch pictures/videos of these important crops.
3. Learners draw and colour the important crops in their community.
4. Learners talk about caring for crop plants.
5. Protect work: Learners talk to parents about taking good care of important crops.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.2.2.1. Recognise important plants and animals in the community
indicator_description: Explore the important animals in the community
examplars:
1. Learners mention some domestic animals in the community and talk about why they are important.
2. Learners observe some of the animals in the community and how people take care of them.
3. Protect work: Learners talk to parents on the importance of being kind to animals by taking good care of them, e.g. feeding, and bathing them.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.2.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of the skills needed to map the environment
indicator_description: Sketch and locate things in the classroom
examplars:
1. Observe the shape of the classroom.
2. Sketch and colour the shape of the classroom, e.g. Square, Rectangle, Round. (Whole-class discussion) .
3. Learners share their work for appreciation.
4. Observe and sketch houses, school buildings and other facilities in the community.
5. Note: Learners develop the skills of using technology tools in observing things around them as a responsible citizen e.g. using a video camera, mobile phone.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.2.4.1. Demonstrate basic understanding of population changes
indicator_description: Explain the need to accept and adapt to a change in the total number of people, as a responsible citizen
examplars:
1. Learners role play problems of a large family size.
2. Represent the number of family members in your house with a bar chart.
3. Explain what happens to the class size when a new learner joins or leaves your class.
4. Learners talk about skills needed to accept and adjust to a change in the total number of people in a place, e.g. respect the rights of others, tolerance, sharing.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.3.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of religious worship
indicator_description: Explain the importance of worship
examplars:
1. Take learners to a place of worship such as the church, mosque or the shrine to observe the mode of worship or show pictures videos of the various modes of worship.
2. Learners role play demonstrate/talk about the importance of worship at home, school, church/mosque/shrine.
3. Learners sing/recite popular religious songs/creed in the three main religions in Ghana. Learners to sing or recite some simple verses from the Bible or Quran and some sayings from oral tradition. e.g. (i) The Lord's Prayer (Christian), (ii) Al-Fathila (Islam), (iii) Traditional prayer (ATR).
4. Learners write simple texts from the religious songs and recitations.
5. Learners role play or talk about the importance of religious tolerance, e.g. peaceful coexistence, love, humility, sharing, caring, respect, unity.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: Β2.3.2.1. Show understanding of festivals celebrated in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe activities which take place during the celebration of festivals
examplars:
1. Learners talk about festivals they have witnessed: i. Islam Eid-ul-Fitr, Eid-ul-Adha; ii. Christianity - Easter, Christmas; iii. Traditional - Kundum, Odwira, Damba, Bugum, Yam Festival.
2. Learners talk about how the various festivals are celebrated.
3. Learners role play activities that take place during the festivals: merry-making, sharing, family re-union, worship, etc.
4. Learners draw scenes from festivals.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.3.2.2. Investigate the roles of the family and community in their daily lives
indicator_description: Identify things that families do to show their commitment to God
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the religious activities their families perform to show commitment to God: morning devotion, going to church, mosque or the shrine.
2. Learners, in groups, identify the number of times religious activities are performed during the day, week and occasionally.
3. Learners role play what their families do to show commitment to God, e.g. worship, offertory, Sadaqah, Zakat, prayer and fasting.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.3.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of human rights as a citizen
indicator_description: Explain the importance of respecting the rights of others
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the importance of allowing others to enjoy their rights, e.g. peace, cooperation, teamwork.
2. Learners role-play ways of allowing others to enjoy their rights e.g. tolerance, love, respect, etc.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.3.4.1. Narrate the early life stories of the leaders of the three main religions in Ghana
indicator_description: Narrate the story of the early life of the religious leaders in the three religions in Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners mention the names of the religious leaders: Jesus Christ, the Holy Prophet Muhammad S.A.W., Okomfo Anokye, Toha Zie, Egya Ahor, etc.
2. Learners talk about the early lives of these religious leaders: The Lord Jesus Christ; The Holy Prophet Muhammad S.A.W.; A Traditional Leader.
3. Let learners role play some important aspects of the early life of a religious leader.
4. Learners, through questions and answers, to talk about the moral lessons they have learnt from early lives of the religious leaders.
5. Learners explain how they will apply the moral lessons from the religious leaders in their daily lives.
6. Learners role-play the moral lives of the religious leaders. Learners to talk about the need to emulate the good moral values of these religious leaders.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.4.1.1. Recognise individual values and responsibilities in the community
indicator_description: Explain values cherished by individuals in society
examplars:
1. Learners in groups list values and behaviours cherished in the community e.g. respect, teamwork, honesty, hard work, obedience, tolerance.
2. Learners role play the roles of members of the community in the upbringing of its members: education, discipline, counselling, etc.
3. Learners role play the following: i. Patriotism, ii. Tolerance, iii. Volunteerism, iv. Love.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.4.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of national symbols
indicator_description: Explain ways to appreciate national symbols, as a responsible citizen
examplars:
1. Learners demonstrate various ways of showing respect to the national symbols e.g. standing while he National Anthem is being played.
2. Learners sing/recite the National Anthem and Pledge.
3. Learners, through role play, to talk about ways they can help to protect national symbols, e.g. obedience, reporting people who destroy the national symbols, respect for the symbols.
4. NOTE: Let learners write the words of the National Anthem and the Pledge and learn to sing/recite.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.4.3.1. Exhibit knowledge of conserving energy
indicator_description: Describe ways of conserving energy in the home, school and community
examplars:
1. Guide learners, through questions and answers, to explain energy conservation e.g. the prevention of the wasteful usage of energy.
2. Guide learners, through think-pair-share, to talk about strategies for energy conservation, e.g. using solar or wind energy, reducing consumption (e.g. turning off lights), recycling.
3. Describe conserving energy in their homes or in the community, using small group drama.
4. Learners talk about ways of conserving energy, e.g. turning off the light, television, iron and fan after use.
5. Leaners sing and recite rhymes on how energy can be conserved in the community.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.4.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the importance of farming in Ghana
indicator_description: Explain the importance of farming in Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners talk about various farming activities in Ghana.
2. Learners visit and observe different farm activities: poultry farm, crop farm, flower garden, etc.
3. Learners in groups talk about importance of farming in their community.
4. Learners draw the main farm activity in their community.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.4.4.2. Recognise types of simple agricultural tools
indicator_description: Identify the types of simple agricultural tools and their uses
examplars:
1. Learners observe simple agricultural tools and talk about their uses.
2. Learners visit the school garden/farms and observe tools used for digging, pruning, harvesting, weeding, watering.
3. Show pictures/videos of different types of agricultural tools from books and draw them.
4. Learners sketch different agricultural tools.
5. Learners in groups talk about ways of caring for agricultural tools e.g. clean after use, oil metallic tools, keep in appropriate place.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.5.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of Ghana's neighbouring countries
indicator_description: Mention Ghana's neighbours and locate them on a map
examplars:
1. Learners locate Ghana's neighbours on a map of West Africa using the idea of the cardinal points.
2. Learners compose a song/rhyme on Ghana and her neighbours.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.5.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Identify the left, right mouse buttons, use the mouse to perform single and double clicking
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify the parts of a mouse.
2. Learners point out parts of the mouse and call out the name.
3. Learners demonstrate how to use the mouse e.g. the left button and perform single and double clicks in a game exercise.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.5.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of sources of information
indicator_description: Identify sources of information
examplars:
1. Bring real items or pictures of telephone, mobile phone, radio etc., to class.
2. Learners talk about the use of these technological tools.
3. Learners draw the items used for receiving and sending information e.g. mobile phone.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B2.5.4.1. Demonstrate understanding of data collection skills
indicator_description: Demonstrate sending and receiving information from other gadgets
examplars:
1. Guide learners through demonstration to talk about where one can send and receive information e.g. Poster, road signs, newspapers, telephones calls, SMS, etc.
2. Bring sources of data e.g. newspapers or class registers to class; Guide learners to identify data and record them in their books.
3. Through group work, guide learners to talk about the type of information they should pay attention to e.g. information on their education, parents.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.1.1.1 Count and estimate quantities from 0 to 1000
indicator_description: Use number names, counting sequences and how to count to find out 'how many?'
examplars:
1. Skip count forwards and backwards to and from 1000 respectively by 2s, 5s and 10s, starting at 0 or at multiples of 2, 5, 10, 50 and 100; Identify and correct errors or omissions in counting or skip counting sequences.
2. Count by 2s, 5s or 10s, to answer 'how many?' questions about as many as 100 or 1000 objects.
3. Represent the number of objects in a group with a written numeral to 1000.
4. Estimate the number of objects in a group of up to 1000 describe the estimation strategy used; Select an appropriate estimate among all those given and justify the choice.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.1.1.1 Count and estimate quantities from 0 to 1000
indicator_description: Identify numbers in different positions around a given number in a number chart. (1-1000)
examplars: 1. Display a number chart with numbers between 0 and 100 and have learners identify numbers in different positions around a given number. Put learners in convenient groups and give each group a number grid and have them identify numbers in different positions around a chosen number. For example, choose 18 and identify numbers above, below, to the right or to the left etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.1.1.1 Count and estimate quantities from 0 to 1000
indicator_description: Demonstrate a conceptual understanding of place value of whole numbers between 0 and 100
examplars:
1. Develop a conceptual understanding of place value of whole numbers between 0 and 1000 by: explain and show- with bundles of 10s and Is and a tens frame - the meaning of each digit in a 2-digit number.
2. Decompose or partition numbers to 1000 into hundreds, tens and ones (e.g.: 153=100+50+3 or 153=100+53).
3. Explain why the value of a digit depends upon its placement within a numeral.
4. Read a number by indicating the value of each digit (i.e., reading 43 as forty-three and not four three.
5. Partition or decompose numbers to 100 and then to 1000 into equivalent expressions (e.g.: 47=20+20+7, or 30+10+7 etc.).

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Number Operations (Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication and Division)
content_standard: B2.1.2.1 Demonstrate conceptual understanding of operations of addition and subtraction with sums up to 100
indicator_description: Use conceptual understanding of addition and subtraction to add, and subtract numbers to 100
examplars:
1. Add a given set of numbers in two different ways and explaining why the order in which numbers are added does not change the sum.
2. Explain why the difference or sum is the same as the initial number when 0 is added or subtracted from a number.
3. Match a word problem to a missing addend (e.g., 34 + _ = 57), missing subtrahend I (e.g. 27 - _ = 24) or missing minuend 54 = 63 statement.
4. Create an addition or subtraction number sentence and word problem for a number up to 100.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.1.3.1 Develop an understanding of halves and fourths using concrete and pictorial representations [Exclude notation here]
indicator_description: Understand the fraction one-half and one-quarter as the quantity obtained by taking I part when a whole is partitioned into two or four equal parts
examplars:
1. Use concrete objects to explain the fraction one-fourth as the quantity obtained by taking I part when a whole object is partitioned into four equal parts.
2. Use pictorial representations to explain the fraction one-fourth.
3. Use pictorial representations to help learners sort fractions into those that are halves and those that are one-fourths.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.3.1.1 Describe and analyse 2D shapes and 3D objects.
indicator_description: Identify the common features or attributes of a collection of 3D objects (spheres, cylinders, cones, pyramids, cubes) of different dimensions or orientations.
examplars:
1. Recognise and name 3D objects having specific features or attributes (number of equal faces, types of faces, number of corners, etc.).
2. Sort a collection of 3D objects by I or 2 features and explain the sorting rule use.
3. Identify examples of these 3D objects in the classroom and community.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B2.3.3.2 Use standard units to measure lengths, heights, mass and distance around objects.
indicator_description: Recognize the need for standard unit of measurement of length
examplars:
1. Mark a learner's height on the wall and ask 4 learners to use their hands pan to measure the height. Establish the need for standard units.
2. Estimate the measure in non-standard units, and then comparing the estimate with the actual measure.
